financial.note: Major financiers for MINT's global debt profile; interest expense is sensitive to Thai policy rates (~1.25% in 2026) and global credit spreads.
Holds ~3.6% stake; provides capital stability and aligns with local institutional sentiment for Thai blue-chip hospitality.
Government-linked fund holding ~3.1% stake, reflecting MINT's status as a critical national tourism asset.

supply_chain.note: Supplies 100% of cheese and ice cream for 'The Pizza Company' and 'Swensen’s'; protects margins against global dairy commodity volatility.
Primary procurement arm for Minor Hotels Europe & Americas; critical for operational efficiency and ESG-certified supply sourcing.
Suppliers of protein and flour; fluctuations in agricultural commodities directly impact Minor Food's COGS.

equity_cross_holdings.entity: Minor Hotels Europe & Americas (formerly NH Hotel Group)
equity_cross_holdings.note: Contributes ~50% of group revenue; core driver for European RevPAR and ADR growth in 2026.
Surging equity income from this JV has become a significant 'Lifestyle' segment tailwind in 2026.
Targeted for a Hong Kong IPO in 2H26 to raise ~$400M and unlock valuation premiums.

competitors.note: Direct rival across both hotel and food (Centara vs Anantara, KFC/Mister Donut vs Burger King/Dairy Queen).
Competes for high-end hospitality real estate and luxury hotel management contracts in Thailand.
Primary competitor for casual dining and domestic consumer wallet share in Thailand.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Major catalyst for MINT’s 62 properties in the Americas, particularly in host cities like New York and Miami.
Directly correlates with occupancy and RevPAR for Thai-based hotels (Anantara, Avani).
A weaker THB against the EUR inflates translated earnings from the massive European portfolio.
Affects both flight affordability (tourist volume) and the operational utility costs of massive hotel properties.
